name: Folk CRM API
what_it_does: Modern minimal CRM API for managing contacts, groups, pipelines, and notes with a focus on simplicity and personal relationship management.
use_cases: Importing and enriching contact records from LinkedIn or other sources into Folk via agent
Creating and updating pipeline stages for lightweight deal or project tracking
Logging interaction notes and reminders to keep relationship context current
Querying contact lists and groups to segment outreach for agent-driven campaigns
Building lightweight personal CRM automations without Salesforce complexity
not_for: Enterprise CRM deployments requiring deep workflow automation, approval chains, or complex object hierarchies
High-volume sales teams needing built-in calling, email sequences, or advanced reporting
Teams that need a self-hosted or data-sovereign CRM solution
best_when: An agent needs a simple, modern CRM API for personal relationship management or small team use cases without the overhead of enterprise CRM platforms.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade automation, deep pipeline customization, or built-in communication channels (calling/email sequences).

auth.notes: API key passed in the Authorization header. Keys generated from Folk account settings. No scope granularity — key provides full workspace access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan for 1 user with limited contacts and pipelines; API access on paid plans
pricing.notes: Free tier available for evaluation but API access is gated to paid plans. Relatively affordable compared to enterprise CRMs.

performance.rate_limits: Not officially published; estimated at 60 requests/minute based on observed behavior

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are present but minimal — often just HTTP status codes with a brief description. Field-level errors for validation failures are inconsistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Agents must check for existing contacts before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: API surface is relatively small and evolving — endpoints that exist in the UI may not yet have API equivalents
No webhooks available yet; agents cannot react to CRM changes in real-time and must poll
No official SDK or OpenAPI spec published; agents must build against underdocumented REST endpoints
Rate limits are not officially documented; agents should implement conservative throttling to avoid 429s without guidance
Folk is a younger product — API stability and backwards-compatibility guarantees are less mature than established CRMs

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with no scope restrictions. HTTPS enforced. GDPR compliant with EU data residency. No OAuth option. Younger company means security posture is still maturing.
